subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Fungi and Parasites
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Fungi and Parasites.

hook.text: A fungus called Candida lives in your gut right now. If your immune system weakens, it can invade your blood.

buildup.text: Fungal infections range from mild (athlete's foot) to deadly (invasive aspergillosis).

discovery.text: Parasites—malaria, tapeworms, toxoplasma—affect 2 billion people worldwide, mostly in tropical regions.

twist.text: Toxoplasma infects 30-50% of the global population and can alter behavior in infected hosts.

climax.text: Antifungal and antiparasitic drugs are limited—far fewer exist than antibiotics or antivirals.

punchline.text: Ancient enemies. Fewer drugs. Two billion infected. A neglected crisis.

quiz.question: What makes fungal and parasitic infections challenging to treat?
options: Far fewer drugs exist for them compared to antibiotics and antivirals
They don't respond to any treatment
